subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Well-defined, numbered structure with clear introduction, body and conclusion sections
Figures and tables placed appropriately near related text enhance navigation
Logical progression from motivation through data collection, modelling and hypothesis testing
weaknesses: Occasional informal narrative and digressions reduce conciseness
Minor formatting errors (spelling in header, duplicate bibliography entries, missing captions)
Some sections (methodology) are truncated or under-developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the report is coherent, highly organised and flows smoothly between sections, meeting the descriptor for band 4.  Minor presentation slips prevent perfection, but the reader can follow the argument without confusion, and the structure is exemplary apart from a few formatting inconsistencies.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (raw data tables, box-and-whisker plots, scatter plots, regression curves)
Mostly clear explanations that guide the reader through mathematical reasoning
Appropriate use of standard symbols for t, rÂ² and statistical notation
weaknesses: Notation slips (omitted multiplication signs, inconsistent subscripts) affect precision
Some axes and graph titles lack units or proper labels
Minor inconsistencies in terminology (screen time vs. duration on keyboard)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication is clear and uses a variety of representations effectively, justifying band 3.  While notation and labelling are not flawless, the reader can interpret the mathematics throughout.  Overall clarity outweighs the occasional inconsistency.

strengths: Personal anecdote provides genuine context and focus for the exploration
Decisions such as outlier removal and model selection reflect the studentâs initiative and drive
weaknesses: Mathematical technique remains within standard textbook procedures
Independent thinking is present but not pushed to novel or unconventional approaches
Creativity is significant but stops short of truly outstanding innovation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a clear personal approach and significant evidence of independent thinking and initiative, matching band 2.  There is engagement and motivation, but the exploration does not rise to the level of outstanding originality or creativity required for band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on the plausibility of models and the interpretation of the p-value
Clear connection between results and the studentâs understanding of skill acquisition
weaknesses: Critical analysis does not fully address underlying statistical assumptions
Reflection remains brief and occasionally generic, lacking depth in limitations or methodological critique
Concluding remarks reiterate results without thoroughly revisiting the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and some critique, with a clear link to personal understanding, fitting band 2.  However, the reflection is not substantial or consistently critical enough for band 3.

strengths: Relevant application of course-level mathematics (polynomial regressions up to quartic, IQR outlier test, two-sample t-test)
Correct execution of most calculations with plausible results and interpretation of rÂ²
Demonstrates a good conceptual grasp of modelling choices and statistical testing
weaknesses: Errors in the t-test formula and mismatched mean values indicate lapses in correctness
Several regression equations are omitted or miswritten, and units are inconsistently stated
Key intermediate steps (IQR value, pooling rationale) are sometimes incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs course-level mathematics effectively and produces mostly correct results, meriting band 4.  Although there are non-trivial errors in formulas and presentation, the overall sophistication of multiple regression analyses and formal hypothesis testing justifies a score above the middle band.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally relevant question and deciding to test several regression models shows genuine involvement and some independent thought, but the mathematical techniques remain standard textbook procedures.  Hence there is *significant*âthough not outstandingâevidence of independent thinking.

The student demonstrates good conceptual grasp: explains why outliers matter, interprets \(r^2\), and selects a quartic model because it âfits learning plateausâ.  Misinterpretations of intercepts and incomplete discussion of statistical assumptions limit the depth to âgood understanding in partsâ rather than thorough or rigorous understanding.

Calculations of means, variances and \(r^2\) are plausible; plotted regression lines correspond to given equations.  Nevertheless, the t-statistic formula is written incorrectly (minus instead of plus), the test value does not match the stated means, and sample sizes are mismatched, signalling more than âminorâ errors but still leaving most results correct.  Hence âmostly correctâ is the best fit.

Most of the writing relates directly to the research question, yet a noticeable quantity of informal narrative (childhood anecdotes, repeated remarks about being âamazedâ) and duplicated reference entries add length without supporting the mathematics.  Explanations are sometimes verbose while important derivations (e.g. calculation of \(r^2\) or the t-statistic) are condensed or partially omitted, so conciseness is only âmostlyâ achieved.

Correct symbols (\(t\), \(\bar X\), \(s^2\), \(r^2\)) are used, but there are frequent slips: the regression equations miss multiplication signs, the t-formula shows a subtraction under the square root, and subscripts are inconsistently formatted.  The studentâs notation is therefore âmostly appropriateâ rather than consistently accurate.

Explanations are understandable and mostly consistent, but not entirely precise.  For example, the student alternates between âscreen timeâ, âduration on keyboardâ, and âtime on laptopâ, and mis-states the interpretation of the p-value region.  Such inconsistencies reduce overall clarity, placing the work in the âmostly clear with some consistencyâ band.

All mathematics (scatter plot, polynomial regressions up to quartic, IQR outlier test, two-sample t-test) is relevant to the IA syllabus and appropriate to HL AI.  The choice of multiple models and a formal statistical test shows effective application, but the work does not reach âsophisticatedâ depth associated with the top bands.

Tables of raw data, scatter plots, box-and-whisker plot, and four regression curves provide multiple representations that enhance understanding of the data set.  Axes and scales are generally labelled, fulfilling the descriptor of *effective* use, though titles such as âChart Titleâ and the absence of units in some graphs prevent the work reaching a âsophisticatedâ level.

The student comments on the plausibility of each model, reflects on learning curves, and interprets the p-value, providing reflections that go beyond a superficial restatement of results.  The reflections remain brief and occasionally generic, aligning with âmeaningful reflectionâ rather than âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Applying four different polynomial models, using IQR for outlier removal, and performing a two-sample t-test show initiative beyond the bare minimum.  However, the mathematical creativity is limited to trying standard options rather than designing novel methods, so âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ creativity is evident.

The investigation is divided into recognisable sections (introduction, aims, data, analysis, hypothesis test, conclusion), and each section generally follows from the previous one. While there are occasional digressions and language slips, the argument progresses in a logical order: motivation â data collection â modelling â statistical test â conclusion.  The statistical reasoning is sometimes weak, yet the reader can still trace the development without getting lost, demonstrating clearâthough not exemplaryâlogical development.

There is some critiqueâe.g., rejecting the quadratic model as âirrelevantâ, noting linear growth is âabsurdâ.  Yet the analysis does not probe underlying assumptions (independence, equal variances), and calculation errors are not identified, indicating only âsome critiqueâ.

The conclusion links findings to the studentâs understanding of skill acquisition and study habits, showing a clearâthough modestâconnection between reflection and personal mathematical understanding.  The link is present but not deeply explored, matching band 2.

A numbered content list, headings (1.0, 2.0 â¦ 7.0) and sub-headings help the reader navigate.  Figures and tables are inserted near the relevant text and labelled.  Minor weaknessesâduplicate bibliography entries, missing figure captions, and mis-numbered tablesâprevent the structure from feeling âseamlessâ, but overall organisation is clearly above the âbasicâ level and fits the descriptor for a well-organised essay.

The narrative about the studentâs typing struggles motivates the whole exploration, and personal decisions (removing an outlier, selecting 3 h as the cut-off) steer the analysis.  This demonstrates a clear personal approach that drives the work, consistent with band 2.

annotations.comment: The introduction includes lengthy background details and informal language that are not directly linked to the mathematical inquiry. Trim to maintain conciseness.
The methodology description is cut off mid-sentence under section 2.0. Extend and complete the explanation of variable definitions and analytic tools.
The sampling method ârandom systematic samplingâ is named but not explained mathematically. Describe how the interval was determined and why bias was minimized.
The 3 h cutoff between low/high usage groups is introduced without justification. Explain why this threshold was chosen statistically or from literature.
Table 3.0 on raw data lacks a descriptive caption. Add a title explaining variables, units and sample size for standalone clarity.
Data are split between two tables (pages 6 and 7), making it hard to compare full sample. Combine into one continuous table or clearly label parts.
The header title contains a spelling mistake âINTERPRETIONâ instead of âINTERPRETATION.â Correcting this improves professionalism and clarity.
The exploration topic is well-defined and directly relates to the research aim, providing a clear focus from the outset.
The Table of Contents effectively lists sections but omits page numbers, which hampers quick navigation. Include page references for each entry.
Some TOC entries are formatted inconsistently (e.g., bold vs. plain). Standardize font and indentation to improve presentation coherence.
The personal anecdote in the introduction demonstrates genuine engagement and context for the study.
Caption reads âBox and plotâ instead of âBox-and-whisker plot,â which is standard terminology. Revise caption for accuracy.
Link the descriptive narrative (âlearning barrierâ) back to coefficients and curvature of the quartic curve for a deeper analytical reflection.
The t-test formula uses a minus between variances under the root. It should be $$\sqrt{\frac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\frac{s_2^2}{n_2}}$$ to correctly pool variances.
The numerator of the t-statistic shows 42.33â58.85, but earlier mean of group 1 was 32.33. Recalculate the t-value with correct group means.
The interpretation states the p-value lies in the critical region â(>0.05),â which contradicts the fact p<0.05. Revise to correctly interpret p-value significance.
The conclusion reiterates results but does not explicitly revisit the research question or discuss limitations. Strengthen by linking back and noting assumptions.
The bibliography contains duplicate and inconsistently formatted entries. Remove duplicates and apply a consistent style (APA or MLA).
The raw data table on page 7 displays unusual notation (e.g. \overline{72}, fractions). Standardize numerical formatting for consistency.
This table has no caption. Add a concise title explaining contents and units so it can be understood without surrounding text.
The IQR calculation line reads âIQR: -â with no result. Provide the computed value to complete the step-by-step process.
The procedural list for IQR is helpful but could be integrated into equations for clarity rather than stand-alone text.
The outlier boundary is set to â126<x<530 but units are not restated. Always include units (minutes) to avoid ambiguity.
The linear model equation omits ây =â making it unclear which variable is predicted. Write explicitly $$y = 0.121x + 21.922$$.
The rÂ² value is given to excessive precision. Round to three decimal places (e.g. 0.530) to maintain readability.
The quadratic regression formula is miswritten; the x term is missing. It should read $$y=-0.000244x^2+0.229528x+13.561133$$.
Interpreting the deterioration after 8 h in minutes (470) is confusing. Convert minutes to hours or state both units.
The cubic regression section lacks the explicit equation. Present the function to support your interpretation of its behavior.
The rÂ² value is shown to eight decimal places. Round to three significant figures (e.g. 0.603) for a more professional presentation.
The quartic regression discussion mentions the y-intercept but does not provide the equation. Include the full model to justify your interpretation.
Some bibliography entries lack retrieval dates or show inconsistent punctuation. Standardize format to improve credibility.
